symptoms: Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP
NMI watchdog: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 15
INFO: rcu_sched detected stalls on CPUs/tasks: { 16 17 25 27 28 33 42}

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for lockup messages
workflow.observation: Hard LOCKUP messages and kernel panic

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace for all CPUs to find lock contention
workflow.action: bt -a | grep "exception RIP: native_queued_spin_lock_slowpath" -A3 | awk '$(NF-1)~/RDI:/&&$NF~/ffffffffb0207084/{print $(NF-1) " " $NF}' | uniq -c
workflow.observation: 51 CPUs waiting on tasklist_lock at address ffffffffb0207084

workflow.thought: Analyze binary representation of lock value
workflow.observation: Bits set at positions 19, 16, 0 indicating lock state

workflow.thought: Examine specific k_itimer structure's it_lock spinlock
Analyze binary representation of it_lock value
Get address of k_itimer's it_lock field
Get address of k_itimer's entry field

root_cause_analysis: We are trying to take the task_list_lock in posix_cpu_timer_del() with the k_itimer's ->it_lock spinlock having been taken in itimer_delete(). However the tasklist_lock is being held by another task that is getting stuck in run_posix_cpu_timers() waiting on the ->it_lock spinlock that we are holding.DEADLOCK
The root cause is that RHEL7 is missing the patch from upstream commit 3d7a1427e4ce: posix-timers: Use sighand lock instead of tasklist_lock on timer deletion

Since timer deletion doesn't actually need tasklist_lock, with this patch, the task that is holding the timer->it_lock spinlock and calling posix_cpu_timer_del() no longer needs to try to take tasklist_lock hence deadlock of this sort no longer happens.
